chunks.text: Course: EE16B | EECS at UC Berkeley Skip to main content Home Academics Courses EE16B EE 16B. Designing Information Devices and Systems II Catalog Description: This course is a follow-on to Electrical Engineering 16A, and focuses on the fundamentals

Engineering 16A, and focuses on the fundamentals of designing and building modern information devices and systems that interface with the real world. The course sequence provides a comprehensive introduction to core EECS topics in circuit design, s

roduction to core EECS topics in circuit design, signals, and systems in an application-driven context. The courses are delivered assuming mathematical maturity and aptitude at roughly the level of having completed Math 1A-1B, and are aimed at enter

aving completed Math 1A-1B, and are aimed at entering students as well as non-majors seeking a broad introduction to the field. Units: 4.0 Prerequisites: Electrical Engineering 16A, Designing Information Devices and Systems I Credit Restrictions: Stu

ion Devices and Systems I Credit Restrictions: Students will receive no credit for Electrical Engineering 16B after completing Electrical Engineering 20 or 40. Formats: Fall: 3.0 hours of lecture, 2.0 hours of discussion, and 3.0 hours of laboratory

hours of discussion, and 3.0 hours of laboratory per week Grading basis: letter Final exam status: Written final exam conducted during the scheduled final exam period Also listed as: EL ENG 16B Class homepage on inst.eecs General Catalog listing Rel
homepage on inst.eecs General Catalog listing Related Areas: Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS) Signal Processing (SP)
